_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket index, measuring average prices of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice with 2015 as the baseline (100), currently stands at 132, reflecting a 32% increase since 2015. This rise is driven by fluctuations in individual commodities rather than a uniform price change, indicating an uneven climb over the period. The index combines diverse soft commodities whose prices can move abruptly due to localized factors. Recent gains have been significant enough to raise the basket well above its 2015 level.
notes.movers: Among the components, coffee prices surged the most, up 93% since 2015, followed by orange juice at +22%, while wheat and sugar saw more modest increases of 7% and 5%, respectively. These price movements reflect the thinly traded soft commodity markets, where supply shocks like weather events and crop diseases—such as frost and drought affecting arabica coffee beans, and citrus greening or hurricanes impacting orange juice—play a decisive role. This pattern points to supply-side disruptions rather than broad-based inflationary pressures.
notes.why: The overall increase in the breakfast basket is largely driven by a few weather-sensitive crops facing production challenges. Given the nature of these agricultural inputs, price elevations may persist for years, as recovery from adverse weather and disease impacts can be slow and uncertain in these fragile markets.
